mnemonics: CACHE: Closer Access, Cheaper than CPU registers, Helps Efficiency
keyPoints: It stores frequently accessed data from main memory to reduce access latency.
It operates on the principle of locality (temporal and spatial).
It is smaller and faster than main memory (RAM) but larger and slower than CPU registers.
commonMistakes: Assuming cache size is the only factor for performance (miss rate and hit time are crucial).
Believing a larger cache always results in better performance (can increase hit time and cost).
